meta.name: PHQ-A Adolescent Depression Screen
meta.description: The PHQ-A is an adolescent-adapted version of the PHQ-9 depression screening tool, using age-appropriate language to assess depressive symptoms in teenagers, plus two functional impairment items.
meta.author: Johnson JG, Harris ES, Spitzer RL, Williams JBW (2002)
meta.reference: Johnson JG, et al. The Patient Health Questionnaire for Adolescents. J Adolesc Health. 2002;30(3):196-204.
meta.instruction: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by any of the following problems?

questions.text: Feeling down, depressed, irritable, or hopeless
Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Trouble falling asleep, staying asleep, or sleeping too much
Poor appetite, weight loss, or overeating
Feeling tired, or having little energy
Feeling bad about yourself, feeling that you are a failure, or that you have let yourself or your family down
Trouble concentrating on things like school work, reading, or watching TV
Moving or speaking so slowly that other people noticed, or being so fidgety or restless that you move around more than usual
Thoughts that you would be better off dead, or of hurting yourself in some way
In the past year, have you felt depressed or sad most days, even if you felt okay sometimes?
Has being bothered by these problems made it difficult for you to do your work, take care of things at home, or get along with other people?

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your PHQ-A score falls within the normal range, indicating that you are not currently experiencing significant depressive symptoms. Most adolescents have occasional down days, and your responses suggest healthy emotional functioning overall.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle with regular physical activity and balanced nutrition
Keep up with hobbies, sports, and social activities that bring you joy and fulfillment
Talk to a trusted adult such as a parent, teacher, or school counselor if you ever start feeling persistently down

ranges.label: Mild Depression
ranges.description: Your score suggests you may be experiencing mild depressive symptoms that are worth monitoring. While these feelings may not yet significantly disrupt your daily routine, they deserve attention to prevent them from worsening over time.
suggestions: Talk to a parent, teacher, or school counselor about how you have been feeling lately
Stay physically active through exercise, sports, or outdoor activities, as movement helps improve mood
Maintain a consistent sleep schedule by going to bed and waking up at the same time each day
Stay connected with friends and family rather than isolating yourself when feeling low

ranges.label: Moderate Depression
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate depressive symptoms that are likely affecting your school performance, friendships, and daily activities. At this level, professional support can make a real difference in helping you feel better and regain your energy.
suggestions: Talk to a trusted adult about what you are going through so they can help you get the right support
Ask your parent or guardian to schedule an appointment with a school counselor or mental health therapist
Stay physically active even in small ways, as regular exercise has been shown to reduce depressive symptoms
Try to maintain a daily routine with consistent mealtimes, sleep, and study periods
Limit excessive social media use and screen time, especially before bedtime

ranges.label: Moderately Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderately severe depressive symptoms that are significantly impacting your ability to function at school and at home. These symptoms are serious and warrant prompt professional evaluation and treatment.
suggestions: Please talk to a parent, teacher, or school counselor as soon as possible about how you are feeling
A mental health professional such as a therapist or psychiatrist can provide effective treatment for depression
If you are having thoughts of hurting yourself, tell a trusted adult right away or call the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
Remember that depression is a treatable medical condition, not a personal weakness or character flaw
Avoid using alcohol, drugs, or other substances to cope with your feelings

ranges.label: Severe Depression
ranges.description: Your score indicates severe depressive symptoms that require immediate professional attention. At this level, depression can profoundly affect every aspect of your life and may involve thoughts of self-harm. Please reach out for help right away.
suggestions: Please tell a parent, teacher, or trusted adult about how you are feeling immediately and ask for help today
If you are having thoughts of suicide or self-harm, call the 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline right now by dialing or texting 988
You are not alone and you deserve help. Depression at this level is a serious but very treatable condition
Ask a trusted adult to help you connect with a mental health professional for an urgent evaluation
